sast_review.reason: The custom SecureXMLParser attempts to disable external entities via self.set_feature(self.FEATURE_EXTERNAL_ENTITIES, False), but XMLParser does not have a set_feature method or FEATURE_EXTERNAL_ENTITIES constant. This will raise an AttributeError when SecureXMLParser.__init__ is called, meaning the parser is not actually secured. The code does not use defusedxml or any other effective mitigation.

raw.summary: The code attempts to disable external entities by subclassing XMLParser and calling set_feature, but this API does not exist in Python's standard library. The custom SecureXMLParser will raise an AttributeError at runtime, leaving XML parsing vulnerable to XXE attacks. Additionally, the path validation using os.path.abspath is flawed because it does not properly handle symlinks and the prefix check is case-sensitive on Windows.

sast_review.reason: The custom SecureXMLParser attempts to disable external entities via self.set_feature(self.FEATURE_EXTERNAL_ENTITIES, False), but XMLParser does not have a set_feature method or FEATURE_EXTERNAL_ENTITIES constant. This will raise an AttributeError when SecureXMLParser.__init__ is called, meaning the parser is not actually secured. The code does not use defusedxml or any other effective mitigation.
